name: Twin City Bank
description_short: Twin City Bank is a locally owned and operated community bank in Longview, WA, offering checking, savings, loans, and specialized cannabis business banking services.
description_long:
Twin City Bank began operations in April 2000, founded by local business leaders to serve the Longview and Kelso communities in Washington. The bank positions itself as the last remaining true community bank in its market, operating as an alternative to national and regional banks and credit unions. It has maintained its locally owned and operated status throughout its 25-year history.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial products including checking, savings, and money market accounts; IRAs and time certificates; online and internet banking; merchant services; and credit cards. For business customers, Twin City Bank specializes in commercial real estate loans, construction financing, term financing, and operating lines of credit. The bank is an authorized SBA lender, enabling it to facilitate government-backed small business loans. A notable service offering is dedicated cannabis business banking with personalized customer service and low monthly maintenance fees.

Twin City Bank differentiates itself through personalized service, competitive rates, and flexible loan terms including interest-only options. The bank emphasizes reliable access, friendly support, and secure banking practices. Their tagline "There's a Difference" reflects their positioning as a community-focused alternative to larger financial institutions, with experienced and dedicated staff members serving retail and small to medium-sized business customers.

As a traditional community bank, Twin City Bank operates within standard banking hours (Monday-Friday, 9am-5:30pm lobby; 8:30am-5:30pm drive-through) and maintains a physical presence at a single location in Longview. The bank does not advertise specialized consumer credit products like personal loans, emergency cash, or credit repair services. Customers seeking 24/7 digital-only banking or non-traditional lending products would need to consider alternative institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Authorized SBA lender, enabling access to government-backed small business financing
Specialized cannabis business banking with personalized service and low monthly maintenance fees
25 years of community banking history with continuous local ownership and operation
Competitive rates and flexible loan terms including interest-only options
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, IRAs, merchant services, and credit cards
Commercial real estate and construction financing specialization for business customers
Direct customer support available via phone with security verification protocols
cons: Limited to single physical location in Longview, WA; no branch network or national reach
Standard banking hours only (closed weekends and holidays); no 24/7 customer service advertised
Website content does not specify APY rates, loan terms, or fee structures for comparison
No mention of digital-only accounts, mobile app features, or advanced fintech capabilities
Scammer impersonation problem noted on homepage, suggesting potential security awareness issues in the community
best_for: Small and medium-sized business owners seeking personalized SBA lending and commercial real estate financing
Cannabis industry businesses requiring specialized banking services with dedicated support
Longview and Kelso, WA residents wanting to support local community banking
Customers prioritizing personal relationships and local decision-making over digital convenience
diagnosis: Twin City Bank is best for small-to-medium business owners and Longview/Kelso residents seeking personalized banking with local decision-making, particularly those in construction, commercial real estate, or cannabis industries. The main caveat is limited geographic reach (single location only) and standard banking hours, making it unsuitable for customers requiring 24/7 access, national branch networks, or digital-first banking experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
